configOptions.description: Choose an approval and sandboxing preset for your session
configOptions.name: Approval Preset

options.description: Codex can read files in the current workspace. Approval is required to edit files or access the internet.
options.name: Read Only

options.description: Codex can read and edit files in the current workspace, and run commands. Approval is required to access the internet or edit other files. (Identical to Agent mode)
options.name: Default

options.description: Codex can edit files outside this workspace and access the internet without asking for approval. Exercise caution when using.
options.name: Full Access

configOptions.description: Choose collaboration behavior (Default or Plan mode)
configOptions.name: Collaboration Mode

configOptions.description: Choose which model Codex should use
configOptions.name: Model

options.description: Frontier model for complex coding, research, and real-world work.
options.name: GPT-5.5

options.description: Strong model for everyday coding.
options.name: gpt-5.4

options.description: Frontier agentic coding model.
options.name: gpt-5.2-codex

options.description: Codex-optimized flagship for deep and fast reasoning.
options.name: gpt-5.1-codex-max

options.description: Small, fast, and cost-efficient model for simpler coding tasks.
options.name: GPT-5.4-Mini

options.description: Coding-optimized model.
options.name: gpt-5.3-codex

options.description: Ultra-fast coding model.
options.name: GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark

options.description: Optimized for professional work and long-running agents.
options.name: gpt-5.2

options.description: Optimized for codex. Cheaper, faster, but less capable.
options.name: gpt-5.1-codex-mini

configOptions.description: Choose how much reasoning effort the model should use
configOptions.name: Reasoning Effort

options.description: Fast responses with lighter reasoning
options.name: Low

options.description: Balances speed and reasoning depth for everyday tasks
options.name: Medium

options.description: Greater reasoning depth for complex problems
options.name: High

options.description: Extra high reasoning depth for complex problems
options.name: Xhigh

prompt.text: Create a minimal deterministic plan for a Conduit E2E proof. Do not mention private paths, credentials, users, dates, machines, or external services. Return a short plan with the exact heading CONDUIT_E2E_CAPTURED_TERMINAL_PLAN.

terminalPlan.text:
# CONDUIT_E2E_CAPTURED_TERMINAL_PLAN

**Summary:** Produce one deterministic terminal proof that shows the Conduit E2E flow from clean start to explicit success, using fixed inputs, fixed ordering, and a single canonical command sequence with no optional branches.

**Implementation Changes:** Define a small capture script or command recipe that resets transient state, starts the required process set, runs the E2E scenario, waits for stable completion markers, and emits one normalized transcript whose success lines are exact-match assertions rather than human interpretation.

**Test Plan:** Re-run the same capture twice in the same environment and require byte-equivalent terminal output after normalization; fail the proof if startup ordering changes, retries occur, unexpected warnings appear, or the success marker is missing.

**Assumptions:** The proof is terminal-only, covers one golden-path scenario, avoids live dependencies outside the controlled test boundary, and treats determinism as more important than breadth.

content.text:
# CONDUIT_E2E_CAPTURED_TERMINAL_PLAN

**Summary:** Produce one deterministic terminal proof that shows the Conduit E2E flow from clean start to explicit success, using fixed inputs, fixed ordering, and a single canonical command sequence with no optional branches.

**Implementation Changes:** Define a small capture script or command recipe that resets transient state, starts the required process set, runs the E2E scenario, waits for stable completion markers, and emits one normalized transcript whose success lines are exact-match assertions rather than human interpretation.

**Test Plan:** Re-run the same capture twice in the same environment and require byte-equivalent terminal output after normalization; fail the proof if startup ordering changes, retries occur, unexpected warnings appear, or the success marker is missing.

**Assumptions:** The proof is terminal-only, covers one golden-path scenario, avoids live dependencies outside the controlled test boundary, and treats determinism as more important than breadth.
